segments.s743-g89: بجستند is a past tense (3rd person plural) of جستن 'to seek' and here introduces the main action; the verb precedes the indefinite subject هرکس, a poetic inversion meaning 'everyone/ whoever.' که introduces the subordinate relative clause 'who saw the wall' (دیوار دید) where دید is a simple past verb.
segments.s743-g90: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; بوم و بر is a fixed-pair phrase meaning 'home/abode and land' (here rendered as 'home and land'); شاه شد ناپدید is a small clause where شد (become) is used with the predicate adjective ناپدید 'vanished' — a common poetic order (شد + adjective) meaning 'became vanished' = 'vanished.'
segments.s743-g91: به بیچارگی is a prepositional phrase expressing cause/state ('in misery' or 'in despair'). دست ازان بازداشت is an idiom: دست از X بازداشت means 'he withdrew his hand from X' / 'he refrained from it.' بازداشت here functions as a past action meaning 'held back/withheld.'
segments.s743-g92: همی is a continuative particle ('kept/continued to'); گوش و دل is a paired object 'ear and heart' (i.e. attention and feeling). سوی اهواز داشت places the direction/target before the verb داشت ('had/kept'), so literally 'he kept ear and heart toward Ahvaz' — meaning his attention and affection were turned to Ahvaz.
segments.s743-g93: کزان = 'from that' (ک + ز + ان) indicating origin, followed by شهر 'city/town.' کاری‌گر آید کسی is an inverted clause where آید ('comes') and کسی ('someone') relate to کاری‌گر ('craftsman') — overall meaning 'no craftsman came from that town' (contextually the negative is supplied by the following clause).
segments.s743-g94: نماند is the past form meaning 'was left / remained' (here used negatively with the sense 'there remained none'). چنان کار is 'such work' and بی‌بر is an evaluative adjective 'ill‑fated / without fruit' (i.e. fruitless). بسی is an adverb/quantifier ('much' / 'many' / 'to a great extent') modifying the noun phrase; together the phrase says that many such fruitless works remained.
segments.s743-g95: همی‌جست is an imperfective/continuous past 'kept searching' (همی marks continuous action); استاد آن is the phrase 'that master' (subject) and تا سه سال is a temporal adverbial 'for up to three years' modifying the search — word order places the continuous verb first for poetic effect.
segments.s743-g96: ندیدند is past 3rd plural 'they did not see / they did not find.' کاریگری ('craftsmanship, workmanship') is the direct object; بی‌همال is a compound with the negative prefix بی- and an adjective (poetically 'without equal' / 'matchless' in sense), placed after the noun as a postpositive modifier. The clause means 'they found no workmanship that was without equal.'
segments.s743-g9: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when' introducing a subordinate clause. نامور modifies نامه (adjective before noun: 'famed letter'); آید is the simple present/near-future 'comes/arrives'. به بن is a small prepositional phrase ('to/at the root, base') completing آید.
segments.s743-g10: ز من is a causal/prepositional phrase 'because of me'. روی کشور is a genitival/nominal phrase 'the face (or appearance) of the country' with روی before کشور. شود is the simple present/future 'becomes'; پر سخن is a predicative adjective + noun 'full of speech/talk' describing that resulting state.
segments.s743-g11: از آن پس is a temporal phrase 'from that time on / thereafter'. نمیرم is a negative first-person verb 'I will not die' (classical present with negative particle). که introduces the reason 'because' and من زنده‌ام is the explanatory clause 'for I am alive' (زنده‌ + enclitic ام forming 'I am alive').
segments.s743-g12: که here continues the explanatory sense 'that/for' introducing the content of the claim. تخم سخن ('the seed of speech') is a metaphoric noun phrase, من is the genitive 'my', and پراگنده‌ام (adjective with enclitic) means 'I am scattered/widespread' → idiomatically 'the seeds of my words are scattered' (i.e. my words are dispersed).
segments.s743-g13: هر آن کس که is a standard relative construction 'whoever / every person who'. دارد is the present 'has/possesses' governing the following coordinated nouns هش و رای و دین — three qualities. Note هش here is an archaic/poetic quality (intended 'wisdom' or 'sense') rather than an interjection, and و simply links the list.
segments.s743-g14: پس از مرگ is a temporal prepositional phrase 'after (my) death'. بر من is the prepositional object 'toward me / about me', کند آفرین is the verb phrase 'will make/do “afarin”' i.e. 'will exclaim “afarin” / will praise' (کند = does/makes, آفرین = exclamation/praise).
segments.s743-g15: کنون 'now' sets the temporal frame. از here has the sense 'concerning/about' (from/with respect to) مداین is the place-name Madā'in; سخن نو کنم is 'I will make/compose new speech/account' (نو 'new' modifying سخن, and کنم 'I do/make' as the first-person verb).
segments.s743-g16: صفت‌های ایوان خسرو کنم is a second clause giving the object of composition: صفت‌های (plural of صفت) 'descriptions/attributes' of ایوان خسرو ('Khosrow's iwan'). کنم repeats the first-person verb 'I will make/offer/relate' and ties this clause to the previous one as the poet's declared action.
segments.s743-g57: رسن سوی گنج شهنشاه برد is a compact past‑tense clause whose basic order is 'object + prepositional phrase + verb'. رسن (rope/rein) is the direct object placed before the following directional phrase سوی گنج شهنشاه ('toward the king's treasure'), and برد is the final verb in the simple past (3rd person) meaning 'took/carried'. The bare sequence 'گنج شهنشاه' shows the classical Persian pattern where the possessed noun (گنج) precedes its possessor (شهنشاه).
segments.s743-g58: ابا مهر گنجور او را سپرد functions as 'Subject (name/epithet + apposition) + object + verb'. ابا مهر appears as a two‑word name/epithet (Aba‑Mehr) with گنجور in apposition meaning 'the treasurer/treasury' and thus together act as the subject; او را ('him' + object marker را) marks the direct object; سپرد is the past tense (3rd pers.) verb 'entrusted/handed over'. The object marker را attaches to the pronoun (او را) as standard Persian word order.
segments.s743-g59: وزان پس بیامد به ایوان شاه is an adverbial + verb clause: وزان پس ('and after that') is a temporal connector, بیامد is the past 3rd‑person verb 'he came', and به ایوان شاه is the prepositional phrase 'to the king's iwan' (ایوان = iwan/porch; شاه as possessor follows the possessed noun, a common classical order).
segments.s743-g60: که دیوار ایوان برآمد به ماه is a relative/explanatory clause introduced by که ('that/for'), with دیوار ایوان ('the wall of the iwan') as subject and برآمد (past 3rd) as the verb 'rose/arose'. به ماه ('to the moon') is a prepositional phrase used poetically to indicate extreme height or brilliance ('rose up to the moon'). The possessor follows the possessed (دیوار ایوان) as in classical Persian.
segments.s743-g61: چو فرمان دهد خسرو زود یاب is a temporal subordinate clause plus an imperative. چو introduces the time clause ('when'); فرمان دهد خسرو = 'when Khosrow gives the order' (here the verb دهد follows its noun فرمان and the proper name خسرو appears as the agent). زود یاب is an imperative phrase ('find quickly' or 'quickly obtain'), with زود as an adverb modifying the imperative یاب.
segments.s743-g62: نگیرم برین کار کردن شتاب is an idiomatic statement about not being prevented from hurrying: نگیرم here is negative‑framed first person ('I will not be taken/held back' — an archaic/useful idiom meaning 'I will not be prevented'), برین = بر + این ('upon/for this' = 'in/for this'), کار کردن is the verbal noun phrase 'to do the work', and شتاب ('haste') is the abstract noun defining the manner. Together: 'I will not be kept from hastening to do this work.'
segments.s743-g63: چهل روز تا کار بنشیندم is a temporal duration plus subordinate clause: چهل روز ('forty days') gives the duration, تا introduces the limit ('until'), کار is 'the affair/task', and بنشیندم is a first‑person past form 'I sat down' or 'I remained sitting' — here implying 'I waited/sat for forty days until the matter was set/settled.'
segments.s743-g64: ز کاری گران شاه بگزیندم: ز ('from' or 'concerning') + کاری گران ('a weighty/difficult task' with کاری (deed/task) qualified by گران 'heavy/important'), شاه is the agent 'the king', and بگزیندم is an archaic inflected verb meaning 'chose me' (classical ordering can place the agent after the object phrase). Reading the clause as 'the king selected me for that difficult task' fits the syntax and context; note that older Persian verb forms and enclitic object markers can make endings appear unusual to a modern reader.
segments.s743-g65: چو here is the temporal conjunction 'when' introducing a clause; هنگامه 'tumult' is the subject/agent of the clause; زخم is a genitive noun 'wound/strike' modifying ایوان ('the iwan's wound'); بود is the past copula 'was' (classical past) indicating existence/occurrence. Word order is slightly poetic (چو + noun phrase + بود = 'when there was ...').
segments.s743-g66: This clause states a simile: 'بلندی ایوان چو کیوان بود' = 'the height of the iwan was like (that of) Kiwan (Saturn)'. بلندی is the noun 'height' (note: the provided lemma/senses do not list this meaning), ایوان is the possessed noun, چو is the comparative/simile particle 'like', کیوان is the reference (Saturn), and بود is the past copula 'was'. The order (possessed + possessor + چو + similand + بود) is poetic but follows Persian comparatives.
segments.s743-g67: بدان here is the prepositional phrase 'به + آن' ('to/on that') functioning adverbially; زخم is 'wound/strike' governed by بدان; خشمت 'your anger' is the theme; نباید is the impersonal/modal negative 'one must not / should not'; نمود is the verb 'to show/display' (here used in the sense 'display/show'). The construction means 'one must not display anger over that wound.'
segments.s743-g68: مرا را is the object 'to me' (م + را = 'me' with the object marker); نیز 'also/likewise' adds emphasis; رنجی 'any suffering/pain' is the thing discussed; نباید 'must not/should not' is again the impersonal negative modal; فزود is the verb 'to increase' (here used intransitively: 'to be increased'/'to increase'). Together: 'nor should any further suffering be added to me.'
segments.s743-g69: بدو is the prepositional phrase 'به + او' ('to him'); گفت is the verb 'said' (the provided senses list treated it as 'conversation', so none exactly match the spoken past tense here); خسرو is the speaker's name; که introduces the direct speech/that-clause; چندین زمان 'for many a time' is a temporal adverbial. Word order 'بدو گفت خسرو' (to him said Khosrow) is a common classical inversion.
segments.s743-g70: چرا is the interrogative adverb 'why'; خواهی is the 2nd-person present/future form 'you want / you will' used here as the present 'do you ...'; از من 'from me' = 'of/from me' meaning 'distrust me' or 'have suspicion about me'; تو‌ای بدگمان is a vocative 'you—O suspicious one' placed after the clause for emphasis. The clause therefore asks 'Why do you distrust me?'
segments.s743-g71: نباید که is the impersonal negative modal 'it should not be that.../you must not...'; داری is 2nd-person singular present 'you have/hold' (here 'you maintain' or 'you keep up'); ازین دست is an idiom 'of this sort/kind' (از + این + دست = 'of this hand/kind'); باز 'again' modifies the action. The phrase means 'you must not again keep/possess this sort of disposition.'
segments.s743-g72: به is the preposition 'to/towards/for'; آزرم is here most likely a proper name (person) who would be approached; بودن is the infinitive 'to be' (or a verbal noun 'being'); بیامد is the past 'came' used impersonally with نیاز to mean 'need arose/ came (upon one)'; نیاز 'need/necessity/request'. The phrase as a whole expresses 'be forced to go to Azarm in need' or 'a need to turn to Azarm will come.'
segments.s743-g185: هرآنکس که ... دارد is a relative clause: 'whoever has ...'. هرآنکس = 'whoever'; که introduces the relative clause; او راه دارد = 'has that way/access' (او = 'that/he', راه = 'way/access', دارد = present 'has'). نگاه is not a verb here but the noun 'gaze' which will be governed by the verb in the next segment (i.e. 'let his gaze ...').
segments.s743-g186: بخسپد is a 3rd-person jussive/subjunctive verb meaning 'let ... rest/sleep' (here used with the preceding نگاه: 'let his gaze rest'). برین گاه is an adverbial phrase 'at this time' where بر + ین (an archaic/poetic form of 'این') + گاه ('time'). ایمن ز شاه is a predicate 'safe from the king' (ایمن = safe, ز = poetic 'from', شاه = king). Word order is poetic: object 'نگاه' precedes the verb and the time/adverbial and predicate follow the verb.
segments.s743-g187: دگر here means 'else/otherwise'. هرک is 'whoever/whichever'. یازد is a verbal form meaning 'seeks/tries for/sets upon' (an older poetic verb); the supplied sense-list does not include this precise meaning, so it is marked separately. به چیز کسان = 'toward/for the things of people' (به = to/for, چیز = things/possessions, کسان = people/others). The whole segment means 'as for others, whoever makes for others' things...'.
segments.s743-g188: بود here functions as a past/habitual auxiliary ('was/would be') giving a past or habitual sense to the clause. چشم ما is the subject 'our eye/our sight'. سوی is a preposition 'toward'. آنکس is 'that person' (the one mentioned). رسان is the verb meaning 'cause to reach/bring/turn toward' (here 'was turned toward' or 'fell upon'); in poetic order the verb follows the object. Together: 'our eye would be turned toward that person.'
segments.s743-g129: چوشد is a contracted classical sequence word (چو شد) introducing a temporal clause 'when'. هفت سال آمد literally 'seven years came' — a common poetic way to say 'seven years passed/arrived' (سال + آمد = period + 'came/passed'). ایوان بجای places the building (ایوان) with the following locative phrase بجای 'in/into the place' ('in its proper place'). Word order places the temporal clause first and then the event (the iwan coming into place).
segments.s743-g130: پسندیده is an adjective meaning 'pleasing' or 'approved' that here qualifies the iwan (i.e., 'pleasing to ...'). خسرو is the proper name/title 'Khosrow'. پاک‑رای is an epithet 'of pure reputation/pure‑minded' modifying خسرو; the three words together form the description 'pleasing to Khosrow the pure‑minded.'
segments.s743-g131: مر here is an archaic dative marker meaning 'to/for him/it' (classical usage), so مر او را = 'to him/it'. بسی modifies the amount 'much/plentifully' and attaches to آب داد 'gave water' (آب = water). داد is the past tense 'gave' (not 'justice'). The conjunction و links the next noun زمین (land) which is another gift.
segments.s743-g132: درم and دینار are monetary gifts (dirham, dinar). Each is followed by داد 'gave'. The final phrase کرد آفرین is a light verb construction: کرد 'made/did' + آفرین 'acclamation' = 'praised/exclaimed "bravo"' (i.e., he expressed approval). The repeated و simply links the series of gifts and the concluding act.
segments.s743-g133: همی‌کرد is the habitual/continuous past narrative form meaning 'would do' or 'kept doing' (a frequentative past). هرکس is the indefinite subject 'whoever/everyone.' به + ایوان marks direction 'toward the iwan.' نگاه is the noun 'gaze/glance' governed by همی‌کرد (i.e., 'would gaze').
segments.s743-g134: به نوروز here means 'at/for Nowruz' (Nowruz being the occasion). رفتی is the past form 'went' (here third‑person narrative: 'would go' or 'went on that day'). The following b + دان form the demonstrative direction 'بدان' = 'to that,' and جایگاه is the noun 'place/spot' (i.e., 'to that place'). Together: 'and on Nowruz went to that place.'
segments.s743-g135: کس here with the negative verb ندید yields 'no one'. اندر جهان is a locative prepositional phrase 'in the world'. زخم چونین is a noun followed by the demonstrative/qualifying adjective 'چونین' ('such/like this'), so the phrase means 'a wound such as this'. ندید is the past tense (negative form) of دید — 'did not see'.
segments.s743-g136: نه begins a second negative clause ('nor') and the subject is omitted (understood from the previous clause). از marks source or provenance 'from'. کاردانان پیشین is a plural noun + postmodifier meaning 'the earlier/former experts'. شنید is the past tense of شنیدن and here means 'heard (of)' — i.e. they had not heard of such a thing.
segments.s743-g121: بدانست is a past-tense verb meaning 'realized/understood' and comes before the subject (خسرو) in this poetic word order; که introduces a subordinate reported-speech clause ('that ...'). او راست گفت is a compact clause 'he spoke the truth' (او subject + راست 'true/truth' as predicative/adverbial + گفت 'said/spoke' as verb), with راست functioning as the content of what was said.
segments.s743-g122: This clause states a general truth. کسی is negative/universal here ('no one'); راستی ('truth') is the direct object, marked by the object marker را. نهفت is an adjectival/verbal form meaning 'hidden/concealed' (i.e. 'be hidden' or 'is hidden'), so the whole line says that nobody causes the truth to be hidden. The verb-form نیارد in the middle is archaic here and is effectively negative/ability-related in context (not reflected in the provided sense list).
segments.s743-g123: رها کرد is a compound: رها (free/release) + کرد (do/make) = 'he released'. هر کو is a literary/poetic relative ('whoever' / 'anyone who'); به زندان marks destination ('into prison' — به + زندان). بدند is an archaic plural verb meaning 'they put/gave (into)' in this context, so the whole clause means 'he freed whoever they had put into prison.'
segments.s743-g124: This short clause names the people affected and the result: بداندیش‌گر is a compound noun 'ill‑wisher / malicious‑thinker' and بی‌گزندان is a predicative adjective 'unharmed / without injury'. The final بدند again is an archaic verbal form indicating what was done to them (here: they were let go / left unharmed).
segments.s743-g125: مر is an archaic/dialectal clitic meaning 'to me'/'to me' or marking the indirect object; here it introduces the beneficiary. او را (object-marked pronoun) and یکی 'one' introduce the coin; بدره is a poetic adjective 'full-moon' (a common praise-image for beauty or roundness) qualifying دینار. داد is the past verb 'gave.' Word order is typical of Persian poetry where indirect object markers and adjectives can appear before the verb.
segments.s743-g126: به زندانیان is a prepositional phrase 'to the prisoners' (به = to/for + زندانیان = prisoners). چیز بسیار is a noun plus quantifier 'many things' (چیز = thing, بسیار = many / much). داد is the past-tense verb 'gave,' so the clause straightforwardly means 'he gave many things to the prisoners.'
segments.s743-g127: بران is a prepositional/adverbial particle 'upon that / concerning that' and heads the phrase. کار شد is a light-verb construction ('the affair became/happened'); روزگار دراز is a noun phrase 'a long time' (روزگار = time/fate, دراز = long). In combination the segment states that the matter lasted a long time.
segments.s743-g128: به کردار آن is a prepositional phrase 'by/according to that deed/action'; شاه را uses the object/contrast marker را to focus the king as the affected party. بد نیاز is a compound meaning 'in bad need / severely needy / badly distressed.' Syntactically this says that, because of that conduct, the king was left in dire need.
segments.s743-g33: This is a clipped, archaic phrasing meaning “the king said to him, ‘accept this from me’.” بدو is the prepositional clitic “to him/for him,” گفت is the past-tense verb “said” (here represented by a lemma that lists conversation-related senses), and شاه is the subject “the king.” این ز من درپذیر is an imperative clause: این ‘this’, ز من ‘from me’, and the verb is the old compound درپذیر (split here as در + پذیر) meaning “accept/receive.”
segments.s743-g34: سخن هرچ گویم ز من یاد گیر is an exhortation addressed to the addressee: سخن ‘words’, هرچ ‘whatever’, گویم is the speaker’s verb-form ‘I say’ (used inside the relative phrase “whatever I say”), ز من ‘from me’, and یاد گیر is an idiomatic imperative: یاد ‘remembrance’ + گیر (imperative of گرفتن used causatively) = “take to heart / remember.”
segments.s743-g35: یکی جای خواهم که فرزند من = “I want one place/position such that…” یکی here has the indefinite/certain sense “a certain/one,” جای is “place/position,” خواهم is the first-person present/future form “I want/I will have,” and که introduces the purpose/relative clause ‘that,’ with فرزند من ‘my son’ as its subject.
segments.s743-g36: همان تا دو صدسال پیوند من = “(that) so that — for two hundred years — my bond/line.” همان here points back to the place/position (“that same [place]” or “thereby”), تا is the temporal/extent marker “until/for,” دو صدسال is “two hundred years,” and پیوند من is a possessive phrase “my bond/lineage” (literally “my link/bond”), expressing the hoped-for continuance of descent.
segments.s743-g37: نشیند بدو در نگردد خراب is a hortative/optative construction. نشیند is a classical third-person present/subjunctive form meaning “may it sit / may it stand / may it be set,” بدو is “for him,” and در + نگردد + خراب here together express “and may it not become ruined inside/of it” — نگردد is from گشت/گردیدن meaning “become,” and خراب is the adjective “ruined.” The particle در is functioning in an older compounded verbal/locative sense and does not carry the simple modern sense “in” as a separate preposition here (hence its sense is not directly listed).
segments.s743-g38: This is a simple list of causes: ز باران و از برف و از آفتاب = “from/by rain and from snow and from the sun,” where ز/از mark agency/causation and link the weather elements that might otherwise damage the place.
segments.s743-g39: مهندس پذیرفت ایوان شاه = “The architect/engineer accepted the king’s iwan.” مهندس is the agent “engineer/architect,” پذیرفت is the past-tense verb “accepted/took on” (third-person singular), and ایوان شاه is a possessive noun phrase “the king’s iwan/porch” (noun + possessor follows in Persian poetic order).
segments.s743-g40: بدو گفت من دارم این دستگاه = “he said to him, ‘I have this device/plan.’” بدو = “to him,” گفت = “said,” من = subject “I,” دارم = present “I have,” این = “this,” and دستگاه is “apparatus / plan / system” (here meaning the architect’s design or method). The clause is direct speech reported immediately after the narrative verb گفت.
segments.s743-g105: چنین (thus) precedes the reporting verb to mark the manner of speech; گفت is the past tense verb 'said' and is followed by the subject رومی (the Roman) in the typical classical Persian V‑S order. که‌گر is a fused subordinating expression (که + گر) meaning 'if' and introduces the conditional clause that follows, selecting a subjunctive/irrealis verb form.
segments.s743-g106: فرستد is the subjunctive/irrealis form 'send' used inside the conditional introduced by که‌گر; مرا is the object 'me' (the clitic appears here as م and the object marker را follows), را is the definite object marker attached after the clitic, با introduces accompaniment ('with'), یکی is an indefinite numeral/pronoun 'one/a certain', and استوار is an adjective 'steadfast' modifying that one (implied 'man' or companion).
segments.s743-g107: بگویم is a first‑person present/subjunctive 'I will say / I should say' used to report what the speaker intends to tell; the following ب + دان here functions as the prepositional 'به + آن' → 'to that', so بدان کاردان = 'to that skilled/expert man'; پوزشم is a noun with a first‑person suffix (پوز + -م) meaning 'my pûz' (lit. my face/mien), serving as the object of بگویم.
segments.s743-g108: به + پوزش = 'by/with the apology'; بجا آید is an impersonal/contracted predicate meaning 'it will be appropriate / it will avail' (آید = 'comes/happens'); افروزشم here appears as a name or formula appended to the clause (the lemma allows reading as a personal name or vocative), closing the thought. The whole unit states that the speaker's apology will make things right.
segments.s743-g109: فرستاد is the simple past 'he sent'; و links clauses 'and'; رفتند is the past plural 'they went/left'; ز is the poetic preposition 'from'; ایوان شاه 'the king's iwan/portico' is the place from which they departed. The verb precedes the subject/phrase as typical in epic Persian narrative.
segments.s743-g110: گران‌مایه is a prefixed adjective 'precious/esteemed' placed before the noun استاد 'master/teacher'; با introduces accompaniment 'with' and نیک‌خواه is an attributive noun 'well‑wisher' or 'benevolent one'. The phrase describes who left (the precious master, accompanied by a well-wisher).
segments.s743-g111: همی‌برد is a classical continuous/imperfective form (همی‑ prefix + برد 'took/led') giving an iterative or ongoing past 'kept taking/led'; دانای رومی is the agent 'the wise Roman' in apposition after the verb (V‑S order); رسن 'rein/rope' is the direct object (no را here). The phrase describes continuous action of leading by the rein.
segments.s743-g112: همان مرد را = 'that same man' with را marking it as the definite direct object (or topicalized element); نیز 'also' adds inclusion; با خویشتن 'with himself' uses خویشتن as the emphatic reflexive 'himself/with him', indicating the man accompanied the Roman. Word order places the topicalized NP before the accompaniment.
segments.s743-g25: چو is a temporal conjunction here meaning "when." صد مرد is the subject phrase (numeral + noun) and بیرون شد is the past action "came forth/went out," with the prepositional source از رومیان following to indicate these men belonged to the Romans; classical word order allows the source phrase after the verb.
segments.s743-g26: ز is the poetic form of از meaning "from." The coordinate list names origins: ایران و اهواز (Iran and Ahvaz). وز here is the fused sequence و + ز ("and from"), used before هر میان "every district/region;" this fusion is common in classical poetry. The whole phrase lists the places whence they came.
segments.s743-g27: از ایشان "from them" marks the group they drew from; دلاور is used as a noun/adjective phrase "valiants/valiant ones" and گزیدند is the past plural verb "they chose." The numeral سی (thirty) identifies how many were chosen and is tied to دلاور even though it appears after the verb in the line's sequence.
segments.s743-g28: از آن qualifies the previously mentioned thirty ("of that thirty"). The remainder enumerates the composition: سی دو رومی و دو پارسی = "of those thirty, two Roman and two Persian," with number + noun order (سی دو etc.) listing the parts.
segments.s743-g29: بر خسرو آمد = "came to/toward Khosrow," with بر used classically as the preposition meaning "to/onto/toward." جهاندیده modifies مرد (an adjectival epithet "worldly‑wise/experienced man"); adjective + noun order is normal in classical Persian.
segments.s743-g30: بر او is the dative/prepositional phrase "to him." کار و زخم is a coordinated object "the deed and the wound." بنا immediately follows زخم and functions as a genitival modifier here ("the builder's wound"), and یاد کرد is a light‑verb construction meaning "recounted/mentioned" (یاد + کرد = "made mention/remembered").
segments.s743-g31: گرانمایه is an honorific adjective "esteemed/precious" qualifying رومی (the Roman). که introduces a relative clause modifying رومی. بد هندسی is a short adjectival/adverbial phrase: بد "bad/poorly" qualifying هندسی ("in style/geometry/rhetoric") — together implying awkward or poor phrasing.
segments.s743-g32: به گفتار بگذشت is a classical idiom: به + گفتار ("into speech/into words") + بگذشت ("passed/turned"), meaning "he began to speak/turned to speaking." از پارسی marks the language ("in Persian"); in older Persian construction, از + language indicates the tongue in which one speaks.
segments.s743-g113: بپیمود is a past narrative verb (3rd person) meaning ‘he traversed/reached’ (archaic literary form). بالای کار is a prepositional/locative phrase 'at the top of the work/structure' (بالای = 'on/above', کار here = 'the structure/deed/undertaking'); و simply links the two actions. برش here is a substantival/verb-form meaning 'the cutting' or 'he cut (him)' (broadly 'a cut/cutting') and attaches as the second element of the compound action.
segments.s743-g114: کم آمد is a predicative phrase 'it came few/insufficient' meaning 'there was little / little came of it' (کم = insufficient, آمد = came/present). ز کار is an origin/agent phrase 'from the deed/work' and the doubled prepositions ز ... از ... here emphasize origin: از رسن 'from the rope'. هفت رش is a quantified phrase 'seven رش' where رش is used as a small amount / drops (poetic measure); the whole clause says that only seven small drops came from the rope.
segments.s743-g115: رسن is the direct object 'the rope/rein'. باز is an adverb 'again' (or 'back'); بردند is the past 3rd-person plural 'they carried / they took'. نزدیک شاه is a locative phrase 'near the king' (نزدیک = near, شاه = king). Word order is object–adverb–verb–locative, a common literary arrangement.
segments.s743-g116: بگفت is the narrative past 'said' (used to introduce reported speech). آنک is a relative/demonstrative 'the one who / that which' pointing to a person; با او = 'with him'; بیامد is past 'came'; به راه = 'on the road' (literally 'to/onto the road' but idiomatically 'along the way'). Together they report what was said about someone who had accompanied him on the road.
segments.s743-g117: چنین گفت رومی is a fixed narrative phrase 'thus said the Roman' (چنین = thus, گفت used narratively). که here introduces the following subordinate/conditional clause; ار is the archaic conditional 'if'. زخم کار is a compound 'the wound of the deed' (زخم modifying کار), a poetic expression meaning the injury/consequence of the action.
segments.s743-g118: برآوردمی is a first‑person singular form meaning 'I would bring forth / I would produce' (برآوردن in an archaic/poetic conditional sense). بر سر‌ای is a vocative/locative phrase 'upon/for your head, O ...' where ای marks the vocative; شهریار is the addressee 'O king/suzereign'. Grammatically this is the apodosis-in-waiting introduced by the prior ار ('if').
segments.s743-g119: نه ... نه ... ونه is the standard Classical Persian negative coordination 'neither ... nor ... nor'. دیوار ماندی = 'no wall remained' (ماندی is a past-form 'remained/was left'); طاق = 'arch'; کار = 'the work/structure.' The sequence lists things that no longer stood.
segments.s743-g120: نه من ماندمی is the final negative clause 'nor did I remain' (من = I; ماندمی = past 1st-person 'I remained/would remain' used here narratively). بر در شهریار = 'at the gate of the king' (بر = on/at; در here = 'door/gate'; شهریار = king). The clause emphasises that even the speaker did not remain at the royal gate.
segments.s743-g145: فروتر (comparative adjective) precedes the comparison phrase and means “lower/more lowly.” ز here is the poetic short form of از used for comparison (“than”), so the phrase فروتر ز موبد = “lower than the mōbed.” مهان را is the definite object/topic (“the guests”), marked by را after مهان; the whole segment says you assigned the guests a place lower than the mōbed.
segments.s743-g146: This clause continues the action with an and-implied coordination: بزرگان و روزی دهان را بدی. بزرگان is the group receiving something (“the great ones/nobles”). روزی دهان را literally reads as ‘provision/sustenance (for) the mouth(s)’; روزی here functions as a noun meaning livelihood/food and دهان را is the object marked for emphasis. The verbal sense “you gave” is implied by the parallel to the previous verb, so the clause means you provided for the nobles and gave sustenance to mouths.
segments.s743-g147: به + زیر + مهان = a locative/prepositional phrase “beneath the guests.” جای (place) follows that phrase and takes بازاریان (merchants) as its possessor/subject: ‘the merchants’ place beneath the guests.’ The preposition به here introduces the location assigned to the merchants.
segments.s743-g148: بیاراستندی is a verbal/participial form meaning something like ‘they arranged/put in order’ (poetic/archaic formation), here describing completed action by the artisans. همه کاریان is the subject phrase “all the workers/craftsmen.” Together the clause says that all the craftsmen had set things in order (for the merchants’ place).
segments.s743-g149: فرومایه‌تر is the comparative adjective ‘more lowly’ (lower still) modifying جای (place). درویش is the possessor (‘the dervish’ / poor man). بود is the past copula ‘was.’ Word order places the adjective before the noun phrase for emphasis: ‘the dervish’s place was lower.’
segments.s743-g150: کجا here functions as an indefinite/interrogative-like nominalizer meaning ‘that which/what’ (i.e. ‘what he ate’). خوردش = خورد + ش (root ‘to eat’ with the 3rd-person object suffix ‘-sh’) = ‘he ate / his eating.’ از کوشش خویش بود is a predicate: از = ‘from,’ کوشش = ‘effort/labour,’ خوی + ش = ‘his own’ (classical split: خوی with possessive ش), بود = ‘was.’ The clause states that whatever he ate came from his own exertion.
segments.s743-g151: This phrase places further detail at a lower level: فروتر = ‘lower/still lower,’ followed by بریده (‘cut off/severed’) describing the following body parts. بسی is a quantifier (‘many’/‘much’) here modifying دست و پای (‘hands and feet’). Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect: adjective/position first, then the descriptive participle and the quantified body parts.
segments.s743-g152: بسی again functions as ‘many’ (quantifier). کشته is a participle/noun ‘the slain,’ and افگنده (from افکندن) is a past participle ‘thrown/cast/laid’; together they form a passive/resultative construction ‘many slain were cast.’ در introduces the location and زیرجای (‘in the lower place’/‘beneath’) completes the phrase: many killed were thrown into the lower place.
segments.s743-g153: ز ایوان ازان پس is a sequence of prepositional/temporal elements: ز (a poetic preposition 'from') + ایوان (the place 'iwan/porch') + ازان (contraction of ازِ آن, 'from that/thereupon') + پس ('after/thereupon'), giving an ablative/temporal frame. خروش آمدی places the noun 'خروش' ('a cry/uproar') before the verb آمدی; آمدی here is a poetic past (3rd‑singular sense 'came/rose') used as the main verb meaning 'arose'.
segments.s743-g154: کز is a contraction of که + از meaning 'from which / from' and introduces the cause ('from the voices'). آوازها is the plural 'voices/calls'. دل به جوش آمدی is a light‑verb construction: دل ('heart') + به (here 'into/towards', marking change of state) + جوش ('surge/boiling') + آمدی (poetic past 'came'), literally 'the heart came into a boil' → 'the heart was stirred'.
segments.s743-g155: که‌ای is a poetic vocative combining که/که‌ای (here functioning like 'O you who are...') used to address the addressees. زیردستان شاه جهان is a genitive phrase 'the subjects/subordinates of the king of the world' (زیردستان governs 'شاه جهان').
segments.s743-g156: مباشید is a negative imperative/jussive in second‑person plural ('do not be' / 'be not'). It governs the following predicates تیره‌دل و بدگمان: تیره‌دل ('dark/black‑hearted' here = ill‑disposed) coordinated with و ('and') بدگمان ('suspicious').
segments.s743-g157: هر آنکس که starts a relative clause 'whoever/anyone who'. او سوی بالا نگاه is the clause's object/phrase before the verb: او ('he') + سوی ('toward') + بالا ('up/high') + نگاه ('gaze'). The main verb of the clause is postponed to the next segment (کند).
segments.s743-g158: کند گردَد is a two‑verb cluster where کند (do/auxiliary) combines with گردد ('become') to give a resultative sense: 'becomes' / 'will be'. اندیشه او تباه places the patient/predicate after the verb: اندیشه او ('his thought') + تباه ('ruined'). Together: 'his thought becomes ruined.'
segments.s743-g159: ز تخت کیان دورتر is an ablative/adverbial phrase 'farther from the throne of the Kians' (ز + تخت + کیان = 'from the Kayan throne', modified by دورتر 'farther'). بنگرید is the (polite/plural) imperative/subjunctive 'look' / 'cast your gaze' placed after the adverbial phrase.
segments.s743-g160: هر آنکس که introduces another relative clause 'whichever person who…'. کهتر بود is a predicative clause 'was/is lower (in rank)' with بود ('to be') used statively, and بشمرید is an imperative/subjunctive plural meaning 'count/consider/esteem' — here 'consider every person who is lower.'
segments.s743-g73: بفرمود is a past-tense, honorific verb meaning ‘he said/ordered’ and heads a subordinate clause introduced by تا; تا here introduces the content/purpose clause (‘that / so that’). سی هزارش درم is a quantified noun phrase: سی هزار (thirty thousand) followed by the enclitic ش (his/him) attached to هزار, and درم is the currency (dirhams). Word order is verb → clause → quantified object in literary Persian.
segments.s743-g74: بدادند is a past plural verb ‘they gave’ (perfective). تا again marks purpose/result ‘so that / in order that’. او is the third-person pronoun ‘he’. نباشد is the negative present/subjunctive form of بودن (to be) used to express the intended/resulting state ‘may not be / not remain’; here it means ‘not be’ (not remain). دژم is an adjectival predicate ‘sullen/grim’. The clause reads: ‘they gave (it) so that he would not be sullen.’
segments.s743-g75: بدانست is a past tense verb ‘he knew / he perceived’. What follows (کاری‌گر راست‌گوی) is the noun phrase functioning as the referent: کاری‌گر (‘worker / agent / capable person’) modified by the adjective/epithet راست‌گوی (‘truthful, frank’). In classical style the verb can precede the noun phrase it concerns.
segments.s743-g76: که is the relative/subordinate particle ‘that / who’. عیب is the noun ‘fault / blemish’ and آورد is the past-tense verb ‘brought / brought up’ (here ‘bring up / point out’). مرد دانا is the noun phrase ‘the wise man’ (مرد = man/person; دانا = wise) and به‌روی means ‘before / in the presence of’. Together the clause says that the truthful worker would bring a fault up before the wise man.
segments.s743-g77: که again introduces a relative/subordinate clause ‘who/that’. گیرد is the present/subjunctive form of گرفتن here with a modal/iterative sense ‘seizes / takes hold of’. بران is an old fused prepositional phrase meaning ‘upon that / on it’. زخم ایوان is a genitive construction ‘the wound of the iwan/palace/court’ (زخم = wound; ایوان = iwan/porch/court). شتاب is a noun/adverb meaning ‘haste’, modifying the action (i.e. ‘with haste’).
segments.s743-g78: اگر introduces a conditional ‘if’. بشکند is a subjunctive/present form of شکست/شکستن ‘to break’ (here ‘if he should break/expose it’). کم کند is a causative-like phrase where کم (‘little/fewer’) combined with کند (‘makes/does’) yields ‘makes less / diminishes’. نان و آب literally ‘bread and water’ (here idiomatically ‘means of livelihood / sustenance’). The clause says that if he breaks/exposes that wound, it will cause bread and water to be reduced.
segments.s743-g79: شب آمد is a straightforward past clause ‘night came / night fell’ (شب = night; آمد = came). بشد is an archaic/perfective verbal form ‘it became / it happened’ used here as a light verb to mark the change of state. کارگر ناپدید is the noun phrase ‘the worker (became) vanished’ — کارگر = worker/agent and ناپدید = vanished/unseen.
segments.s743-g80: چنان شد is a fixed phrase ‘it became thus / it came to be so’. کزان is a compound relative/adverbial ‘from which / as a result of which’. پس here means ‘after / thereafter’. کس in the negative context plus ندید functions as ‘no one’ (کس … ندید = no one saw him). او را is the object pronoun marked by را, and ندید is the past negative form ‘did not see’. The whole segment: ‘so it happened that afterward no one saw him.’
segments.s743-g49: فرستد is the narrative/past third-person singular 'sent' in Ferdowsi's epic diction. تنی صد is a numeric noun phrase 'a hundred (bodies/men)', where تنی (literally 'bodies') functions as the classifier for people. بدین is the folded prepositional phrase 'ب + این' (written here as ب + دین), giving 'to this', and بارگاه is the direct object/place 'palace/court' (so the whole chunk = 'sent a hundred men to this palace').
segments.s743-g50: پسندیده is an adjectival epithet 'favored/pleasing' that modifies the palace or the mission as a whole (poetic apposition). با is the preposition 'with', followed by موبد 'mōbed, Zoroastrian priest' and نیک‌خواه 'well-wisher / benevolent' describing that priest; together they mean 'with the benevolent mobed'.
segments.s743-g51: بدو is the prepositional clitic phrase 'to him'. داد is the past of دادن 'gave', taking زان گونه مردم 'people of that sort' as its object; که خواست is a relative clause 'whom/which he wanted/asked for' (که = 'that/who', خواست = past 'he wanted/desired').
segments.s743-g52: برفتند is the past third-person plural 'they went/left'. و is the coordinating conjunction 'and'. دیدند is the past 'they saw'. دیوار راست is the direct object phrase 'a straight/true wall', where راست here means 'straight/vertical' (i.e. a straight wall).
segments.s743-g53: بریشم (barišam) is a noun 'fine cloth/silk' (poetic lexeme). بیاورد is the narrative/past third-person form 'he brought' in classical/epic diction. تا introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that', and انجمن is the noun 'assembly/assembly hall' (the people gathered).
segments.s743-g54: بتابند is third-person plural subjunctive/optative in form here used as a purpose/result 'that they may shine' (from تابیدن 'to shine'/'to turn'). باریک is an adjective 'thin/delicate/fine' modifying تابی 'a radiance/gleam' (تابی used as a noun 'radiance' rather than 'endurance'), and رسن is the noun 'rope' — together describing 'a rope of delicate radiance' or 'a finely gleaming rope'.
segments.s743-g55: ز (poetic for 'from') combined with بالای 'above' gives the locative 'from above'. آن refers back to 'that' (the place/structure). تابداده is a compound participial form 'made to shine / given luster' (تاب + داده), so تابداده رسن = 'a rope made to shine' (i.e. a gleaming rope).
segments.s743-g56: به here introduces the manner/state 'in/with' and combines with پیموده (participial/adjectival 'stretched across, measured out, spanned') to indicate the rope was extended; در پیش is the locative phrase 'in front of / before', followed by آن انجمن 'that assembly' — so the rope was stretched across before the assembly.
segments.s743-g137: یکی before the noun is the indefinite 'a certain / one' (used here as an indefinite article). حلقه is the head noun 'ring'; زرین is the adjective 'golden' following the noun (typical Persian word order can put adjective after or before in poetry). بدی is an unusual item here (literal meaning 'evil') and is used attributively with ریخته (past participle) — together ریخته describes the ring as 'cast/fallen'; the phrase thus reads as 'a certain golden ring, (having been) cast/fallen.'
segments.s743-g138: ازان is the prepositional phrase 'from that / from it' (poetic contraction of از + آن). چرخ‌کار is a compound noun 'wheel-work' used metaphorically for fate. اندر is an adverb / preposition 'in, within' (here linking location), and آویخته is the past participle/adjective 'hung' describing the ring's position. Together: 'hung on/within that wheel-of-fate.'
segments.s743-g139: فروهشته is a past participle meaning 'cast down / hung down / scattered down' and here indicates motion downward from the preceding object. ز is the poetic preposition 'from.' و (here orthographically separated) functions as a connective element in the phrase; in context the sequence marks continuation from the previous clause. سرخ زنجیر زر is a noun phrase 'red chain of gold' (سرخ modifies زنجیر, and زر specifies the material 'gold'). The whole segment therefore describes a gold chain, red in color, hanging down from it.
segments.s743-g140: به introduces a locative or directional relation 'on / upon / at.' هر مهره‌ای is 'each bead' with the indefinite -ی; مهره‌ای is singular indefinite. در نشانده is a perfect participle 'placed / inlaid' (نشانده = set into), with در here indicating the cavity/inset ('in'). گهر is the noun 'gem / jewel' that has been set. The clause literally: 'on each bead a jewel has been set.'
segments.s743-g141: چو here means 'when' (a temporal conjunction). رفتی is an archaic past form meaning 'went' (third-person narrative past in epic usage). شهنشاه is the subject 'the shahanshah / emperor.' بر تخت عاج is a prepositional phrase 'onto the ivory throne' (بر = on/upon; تخت = throne; عاج = ivory), giving the location to which the king went.
segments.s743-g142: بیاویختندی is an older/poetic past form meaning 'they hung up / were hung' (the prefix بیـ often appears in classical Persian verbs; the -ندی ending is an archaic third-person plural past). ز is the preposition 'from.' زنجیر is 'chain'; تاج is 'crown.' Word order is object-final/poetic: 'they hung (or it was hung) the crown from the chain.'
segments.s743-g143: به نوروز 'on Nowruz' sets the occasion (به = at/on; نوروز = Nowruz). چون here functions temporally ('when') introducing the subordinate clause. برنشستی is the past form 'sat (upon) / mounted' (برنشستن = to sit upon/ascend), used in narrative to mean 'he took his seat on.' The following به تخت repeats the locative 'on the throne.'
segments.s743-g144: به نزدیک او is a prepositional phrase 'to his near (place) / near him' (به + نزدیک + او). موبد نیک‌بخت is a nominative noun phrase 'the fortunate / well-fated mobed (Zoroastrian priest)'; in this epic word order the phrase functions as subject or agent moving into proximity. Together: 'the fortunate mobed came near him.'
segments.s743-g161: وزان پس is a fused poetic prepositional phrase meaning 'from that time/after that' (وز = و + ز). تنِ کشتگان را is the definite object: تن 'body' + کشتگان 'the slain' with را marking the object. به راه is a prepositional phrase 'onto/along the road' and attaches to the verb in the next segment.
segments.s743-g162: کزان 'from which' introduces a relative sense ('the road from which/through which one passes'). بگذری is a subjunctive/present-form of 'to pass' (literally 'you/one pass(es)') used here generically. نگاه + کردن is the verb 'to look', but Ferdowsi inverts the usual order: کرد (do) and باید (necessary) surround نگاه, so the clause means 'it is necessary to look' or 'everyone passing by must see them' — a poetic word order for باید نگاه کردن.
segments.s743-g163: وزان پس again marks 'from that time/after that'. گنهگار 'guilty/sinner' and و‌گر ... بیگناه 'or if innocent' form a correlative pair meaning 'whether guilty or innocent' (وگر functions like 'or if / whether').
segments.s743-g164: نماندی is the past negative of ماندن 'to remain' ('did not remain'), placed before the noun as is common in classical Persian; کسی 'anyone' is the logical subject (so: 'no one remained'). نیز here means 'even/also' or adds emphasis. دربندِ شاه is a locative/genitive phrase 'in the king's prison' (دربند 'imprisoned/place of captivity' modified by شاه).
segments.s743-g165: به introduces the indirect object 'to/for'. ارزانیان is a plural noun here referring to the lowly/needy (an archaic/poetic usage); جامه‌ها 'the garments' is the definite plural object placed before the verb, and داد is the past tense 'gave'. نیز 'also' adds that this is one of several kinds of gifts.
segments.s743-g166: ز is the poetic preposition 'of/from' introducing a list of items. دیبا و دینار و هرگونه چیز enumerates what was given: 'brocade and dinars and every kind of thing' (هرگونه + چیز = 'any kind of thing'). The repeated و simply joins the list.
segments.s743-g167: هرآنکس که 'whoever' introduces a relative clause. درویش here denotes a dervish/poor person (context favors 'poor person'). بودی is an older/past copular form 'was'. به شهر 'in the city' gives the location; word order places the predicate before the locative adjunct.
segments.s743-g168: This clause contains an implied noun (like 'thing' or 'gift') that is omitted: 'that there was not (a thing) for him from Nowruz.' که introduces the result/relative content; او را نبودی is an impersonal or existential negative construction literally 'there was not for him' (نبودی = 'was not'), and ز نوروز بهر means 'from/for Nowruz' — i.e., he did not go without a Nowruz gift.
segments.s743-g169: This is a prepositional phrase 'به درگاه ایوانش' (به + noun) followed by the past-tense verbal form 'بنشاندند' (they seated). Word order places the locative/complement before the verb, typical in poetry. 'ایوانش' has the third-person possessive suffix -ش (= 'his/its'). 'بنشاندند' is an archaic/poetic past plural form meaning 'they seated/installed (him/them)'; the direct object is omitted but understood from context.
segments.s743-g170: 'درمهای گنجی' is an object phrase (plural dirhams described as treasure-related) placed before the verb. 'بر' here functions as a preverb/adverbial 'upon/over' connected to the verb, and 'افشاندند' is the past plural 'they scattered/strewed'. The whole segment means they scattered treasure-coins (dirhams) about.
segments.s743-g171: 'پر از بیم' is a predicate meaning 'full of fear' where 'پر' functions as an adjective and takes the preposition 'از' + noun. 'بودی' is the past-tense copula 'was' (classical/poetic form). Word order here places the predicate before the subject 'گنهکار' ('the sinner'). The final 'از وی' gives the cause/target of the fear: 'because of him / of him'.
segments.s743-g172: The verb 'شده' (become / were made) comes first for poetic inversion; it governs the following nominal phrase 'مردم خفته بیدار' — 'the people, (who were) asleep, (were) awake' — indicating a change of state: the sleeping people were roused. The final 'از وی' again gives agent/cause 'by him' or 'because of him.'
segments.s743-g173: 'منادیگری دیگر' is a nominal phrase meaning 'another herald/one engaged in heralding,' with 'دیگر' as 'another.' 'اندر' is an archaic/pre-classical preposition meaning 'in/within,' and 'سرای' here denotes 'house/hall/chamber' (a place). Together they mean 'another herald in the hall/house.'
segments.s743-g174: 'برفتی' is a poetic past of رفتن with the prefix بر- indicating 'went away/entered away' and here functions as the past action 'he went/entered.' 'گه' is an adverb 'occasionally/at times,' qualifying the following infinitive 'بازگشتن' ('to return'). 'به جای' is a prepositional phrase 'to (his) place/station.' The sequence says he entered and at times returned to his place; poetic word order places the finite verb before the adverb and infinitive.
segments.s743-g175: 'که‌ای' is a poetic vocative/relative particle used to address someone ('O you who are...'). 'نامور' is an epithet 'renowned' and 'پر هنر' literally 'full of art/skill' (پر + noun = 'full of X'). 'سرکشان' is a plural/collective form (from سرکش) meaning 'the proud/proud ones' or 'chiefs/rebels' and functions here to locate the addressee among that group; the exact nuance is literary (haughty/proud leaders).
segments.s743-g176: 'ز بیشی' = 'from surplus/abundance' (ز = from/of, بیشی = excess/abundance). 'چه جویید' is an interrogative phrase 'what did (you) seek?' with 'جویید' as the poetic/present-tense or narrative past-form of 'seek' used rhetorically here. 'چندین نشان' is the object 'many signs/tokens,' placed after the verb in typical Persian order. The line asks rhetorically what further tokens the addressee sought from abundance.
segments.s743-g1: This is a prepositional/topic fragment: از ایوان خسرو = “from Khosrow’s iwan/porch.” Classical/poetic Persian often omits the ezafe (-e) between ایوان and خسرو; ایوان خسرو therefore functions as a possessive phrase. کنون is an adverb (“now”) placed before the noun داستان (story/tale) for emphasis; the full object/story word (داستان) is carried over into the next segment where the verb appears.
segments.s743-g2: بگویم is the 1st-person present-subjunctive/optative used as the main verb: “I will tell” / “let me tell.” The conjunction که introduces a relative/clausal clause modifying داستان. پیش آمد is a light-verb construction meaning “occurred/happened” (پیش as an adverb + آمد ‘came/happened’). از راستان is a prepositional phrase “from/of the righteous” indicating where/among whom the event occurred.
segments.s743-g3: جهان is the subject “the world.” The preposition/particle بر introduces the phrase that follows and combines with the verb بگذرد (“passes”); the coordinated nouns کهان و مهان (joined by و) name the classes over which the world passes. بگذرد is the present/subjunctive form meaning “passes by” or “moves on.” Word order is subject + prepositional phrase + verb, typical of poetic Persian.
segments.s743-g4: This is a short rhetorical question. خردمند مردم is a noun phrase where خردمند modifies مردم (a wise one of the people / a wise person among the folk); classical poetry may omit the ezafe. چرا is the interrogative adverb “why.” غم خورد is a verb phrase meaning “to take/receive sorrow” or “to grieve,” here with خورد used in the sense of something befalling/affecting someone (not literal ‘to eat’).
segments.s743-g5: بسی is an adverb here meaning ‘many’ / ‘very (many).’ مهتر و کهتر is a coordinated pair meaning ‘more eminent/older and less eminent/younger’ (or ‘greater and lesser’) and is followed by از من indicating the comparison ‘than I.’ گذشت is the past form ‘passed (by)’ — the clause says many, both higher and lower than the speaker, have already passed.
segments.s743-g6: نخواهم is the negative of the volitive/present-subjunctive ‘I want/will’ and here functions as ‘I will not / I do not wish to.’ The phrase از خواب بیدار گشت is a compound: از خواب ‘from sleep,’ بیدار ‘awake,’ and گشت (become) together mean ‘to become awake / to wake up.’ Thus the whole clause reads literally ‘I will not become awake from sleep,’ i.e. ‘I shall not awaken.’
segments.s743-g7: همانا is an emphatic particle ‘verily’ / ‘indeed.’ که شد سال بر شست و شش is a verbless/nominal clause where شد (‘became/happened’) governs the measure phrase سال بر شست و شش meaning ‘the year came to (the number) shast o shesh (sixty-six).’ بر here marks the numerical extent (‘reached’). Note شست is an archaic/numeric term (classical prosody uses شست for sixty), so the construction denotes the passage of that many years.
segments.s743-g8: نه negates the following predicate (omitted in the supplied word-list), so نیکو بود = ‘was good’ is negated: ‘it was not good.’ مردم پیرکش is a noun phrase ‘people who bear old age’ (i.e. the aged/elderly); پیرکش here denotes those who have grown old. The word order places the negated predicate before the subject for poetic emphasis.
segments.s743-g81: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when'. بشنید is past tense 'he heard' (third-person singular). خسرو is the subject (proper name) that follows the verb here for poetic word order; که introduces the subordinate clause 'that ...'. گریخت is a past intransitive verb 'fled/escaped' and completes the content of the subordinate clause. 'فرعان' in this line functions as the thing/person who fled (not one of the ordinary senses given), so the precise lexical sense is not represented in the provided list.
segments.s743-g82: به + noun (به گوینده) is a prepositional phrase meaning 'to/against the speaker' here (marks the target of the action). بر before خشم marks direction/target 'against' (i.e. he directed something at someone). خشم is the noun 'anger', and بریخت is the past tense 'poured/shed' (third-person singular); together 'بر خشم ... بریخت' = 'he poured (his) anger upon/against ...'. 'فرعان' again appears as a modifier in context whose listed senses don't match the usage and is therefore left unspecified.
segments.s743-g83: چنین گفت is a fixed phrase 'he said thus' or 'he replied so'. کان را is an archaic/demonstrative phrase meaning roughly 'to that one' (را is the definite/object marker). که introduces the relative/subordinate clause 'who/that ...'. دانش نبود is a negative past copula construction 'had no knowledge' (دانش = 'knowledge', نبود = 'was not/there was not').
segments.s743-g84: چرا is the interrogative 'why'. پیش ما literally 'before us / in our presence' (پیش = 'in the presence of' here, ما = 'us'). در فزونی نمود is a verbal phrase where در + فزونی ('in excess/excessiveness') and نمود (past of 'to show') means 'he showed/displayed (excess/arrogance)'; the whole clause asks why that excess was shown before us.
segments.s743-g85: بفرمود is an honorific/polite past verb 'he commanded/ordered'. تا introduces a purposive/subjunctive clause 'so that / in order that'. کار او is the object 'his affair/case' (کار = affair, او = his). بنگرند is present-subjunctive/optative (third-person plural) 'they should look into / examine' and depends on تا after a verb of ordering.
segments.s743-g86: همه رومیان را is the direct object 'all the Romans' (همه = 'all', رومیان = 'Romans', را = object marker). به زندان is a prepositional phrase 'into prison' (به = 'to/into', زندان = 'prison'). برند is the present-subjunctive/imperative plural of بردن 'to take/lead' — in the context of an order it means 'let them take/put (them)'.
segments.s743-g87: دگر here means 'again' (introducing another order). گفت is the verb 'said' but the provided sense list gives only nominal senses, so the proper verbal sense is not listed. کاری is intended as 'a work/task' (here object of آوردید), but the listed senses are for a different lemma so the match is not on the list. گران is an adjective 'heavy/weighty' modifying کاری. آورید is the second-person-plural imperative/subjunctive 'bring!' or 'let them bring' (here a command).
segments.s743-g88: This is a simple coordinated list commanded by آورید. گچ 'plaster', خشت 'mudbrick', سنگ 'stone' are listed with coordinating و 'and'. گران repeats the adjective 'heavy' describing the materials. آورید is the imperative/plural 'bring (them)'. The word order is typical: list of objects followed by the verb in the imperative form.
segments.s743-g17: چنین is an adverb meaning “thus/so.” گفت is a past tense verb (3rd singular) here meaning “said/spoke” and introduces direct speech; روشن‌دل پارسی is a nominal phrase (an adjective روشن‌دل ‘clear/bright‑hearted’ before the noun پارسی ‘Persian’), naming the speaker. Word order is typical: adverb + verb + speaker phrase.
segments.s743-g18: که begins a relative clause (“who/that”) modifying پارسی. بگذاشت is a past tense perfective verb (3rd singular) here meaning roughly “left/let go/departed” (an older/poetic use); با کام دل is an adverbial phrase “with the heart’s desire” (با = with, کام = desire, دل = heart), and چارسی is an adverb “on all sides/in every direction,” completing the manner of the departing action.
segments.s743-g19: که here continues a subordinate/relative clause (“for/that”) connected to the previous sentence. خسرو is the proper name “Khosrow.” فرستاد is the past tense verb “sent/dispatched” (3rd singular). کسها is a plural noun “people/men” (here object of فرستاد). به introduces the destination, and روم names that destination (Byzantium/the Romans).
segments.s743-g20: This segment repeats the preposition به “to” for each destination in a coordinate list. هند and چین are place‑names (India, China); آباد بوم is a compound noun meaning “prosperous/peopled land” or “flourishing region” (آباد = inhabited/prosperous, بوم = land/homeland). The repeated به marks each destination in series.
segments.s743-g21: برفتند is a past tense plural verb (“they went/departed/proceeded”). کاری گران is a nominal phrase ‘a weighty/valuable work/task’ (کاری = a work/task; گران = heavy/precious). سه هزار is a numeric apposition (“three thousand”) supplying the number of persons involved; in Persian numerals often follow the noun phrase they quantify.
segments.s743-g22: ز here means “from” (poetic), هر کشوری = “every/each country.” آنک introduces a further relative specification (“those who/which”), and نامدار is an adjective “renowned/illustrious.” بد before نامدار is irregular here (poetic/archaic); it likely intensifies or participates in an old compound, but none of the provided sense glosses fit exactly, so it is left unanalyzed for sense mapping.
segments.s743-g23: از ایشان means “from them / among them.” هر آن کس is a fixed expression meaning “whichever person / whoever,” and the clause که استاد بود is a relative clause “who was a master” (استاد = master/expert, بود = past of to be). The whole segment sets up a generalization about members of the group.
segments.s743-g24: ز خشت و ز گچ‌بر lists the crafts: “of/at (the) brick (work) and of (the) plasterer/plastering” (ز = of/from, خشت = mudbrick/brick, گچ‌بر = plasterer/plasterer’s art). دلش یاد بود is a common poetic construction: دلش = “his heart,” یاد بود = “there was remembrance,” i.e. “he kept the memory/skill in his heart” (بود is past tense).
segments.s743-g97: بسی is an adverb meaning 'much/often' placed before the noun phrase; یاد کردند is the compound verb 'they remembered/they mentioned' (یاد + کردند). زان = 'از آن' ('of/from that') introduces the following noun کارجوی as the thing being remembered/mentioned. Word order is adverb — object phrase — verb, typical in poetic Persian.
segments.s743-g98: به سال چهارم is a temporal prepositional phrase 'in (or into) the fourth year' placed before the verb. پدید آمد is a two-part verbal expression 'appeared/came into being' (پدید = appear/manifest + آمد = came). اوی is an older/dialectal third-person pronoun 'he' placed after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s743-g99: یکی before مرد is an indefinite 'a certain' (introducing someone). The noun مرد is followed by the adjective بیدار ('alert/awake'). با plus فرهی attaches an attribute meaning 'with nobility' — here functioning like an adjectival phrase 'a noble (lit. with nobility)'. This is a compact descriptive noun phrase preceding the verb.
segments.s743-g100: به خسرو is the recipient prepositional phrase 'to Khosrow'. رسانید is the past tense verb 'conveyed/delivered' (3rd person). ز + و here represent the contracted poetic form 'زو' = از او ('from him' / 'about him'), and آگهی is the direct object 'news/information' — together: 'he conveyed to Khosrow information about him.' Note the flexible word order: recipient — verb — source — object.
segments.s743-g101: هم آنگاه is a temporal adverbial 'at that very moment / immediately then.' رومی بیامد is the clause 'the Roman came.' چو is the conjunction 'when/as,' and گرد here (verbal/verb-like in poetry) indicates approaching or going about — together marking 'as he approached/when he came near.'
segments.s743-g102: بدو = 'to him' (dative), placed before the verb. گفت is the past-tense verb 'said' (he said to him). شاه‑ای is the vocative 'O king' (شاه with the vocative particle ای). گنهکار is an adjective 'sinful' modifying the vocative, and مرد is an emphatic vocative/appositive 'man' — the sequence is a direct address following classical Persian word order.
segments.s743-g103: بگو is an imperative 'tell/say.' تا here is a subordinating particle introducing an indirect question ('tell what...'), so تا چه بود functions like 'what was' in reported question construction. اندرین = 'in this' (an old/dialectal form), and پوزشت is 'your پوز/پوزش' (here meaning 'face/appearance' or better in context 'your excuse/defense' with the possessive suffix -ت 'your').
segments.s743-g104: چه گفتی is a direct past interrogative 'what did you say' (چه = what; گفتی = you said, 2nd person past). که introduces the result/clause 'that...' (subordinating 'that/which'), پیش آمد is a verbal phrase 'came before/ensued' (literally 'came forward' i.e. 'ensued/occurred'), and آمرزشت is the noun 'forgiveness/pardon' (your pardon) — so the clause asks which words led to your being forgiven.
segments.s743-g177: This prepositional phrase 'به کار اندر اندیشه' (lit. 'to/with regard to the matter, in thought') places the topic: 'in matters' or 'when dealing with a task'. Word order is topical (preposition + object + locative 'اندر' + noun). 'باید نخست' follows and gives the modal obligation + adverb: 'one must first...'.
segments.s743-g178: 'بدان' is an exhortative/imperative-like form (ب- prefix + دان 'know') = 'know this' or 'consider it'. 'تا' introduces purpose/result ('so that/in order that'), and 'شود' is the copulative/auxiliary 'become' (present/subjunctive sense). The coordinated predicates 'ایمن و تن درست' are results: 'safe and body-sound/healthy'.
segments.s743-g179: The verb 'سگالید' (an archaic/poetic verb meaning to set about/assail — literally 'set dogs on') is an exhortation to begin vigorously. 'هر کار' ('every task') is its direct object, and the coordination 'و زان پس کنید' ('and from that/thereafter do') uses 'زان'+'پس' = 'after that' with 'کنید' (2pl imperative/present subjunctive) meaning 'do/perform'.
segments.s743-g180: The object 'دل مردم' ('the hearts of the people') comes before the verb 'مشکنید' (negative imperative 'do not break'). 'کم سخن' modifies the manner ('with few/scant words'); word order places the phrase describing how hearts might be broken before the verb for emphasis.
segments.s743-g181: 'بر انداخت' is a compound verb phrase (بر + انداختن 'to throw/overthrow') meaning 'cast down/overthrow'. Placing 'باید' after it expresses necessity ('it must be cast down'). 'پس آنگه برید' is a sequential pair ('then/afterwards, then') with 'برید' (here an imperative/command form) meaning 'strike/cut/press home' — the sequence gives a tactical order: do X first, then Y.
segments.s743-g182: 'سخن‌های داننده' is a genitive phrase 'the words of the knower/expert'. The impersonal/modal 'باید شنید' ('one must hear' / 'must be heard') uses 'باید' + verbal infinitive/presentive 'شنید' to express obligation: one ought to listen to knowledgeable speech.
segments.s743-g183: 'ببینید' is the imperative (2pl) 'see/observe'. 'تا' here introduces the content/result ('so that' / 'to see which...'), and 'از شما ریز کیست' literally 'who is small among you' uses 'از' + pronoun to mark 'among/of you' and places the copula 'کیست' at the clause end (standard Classical Persian word order for embedded questions/relative clauses).
segments.s743-g184: 'که' begins a relative/explanatory clause ('for/that'), and 'بر جان بدبخت باید گریست' is an impersonal construction: 'باید' + verb 'گریست' expresses necessity ('one must weep'), with 'بر جان بدبخت' ('for the life/soul of the unfortunate') as the object/beneficiary of the lament. The past-form 'گریست' functions here as the infinitival/obligatory verbal form common in classical style.
segments.s743-g41: فرو برد is a verbal compound: فرو (a directional/adverbial prefix meaning 'down' or 'deeply') combines with بردن to form 'to sink/lay down' (past). بنیاد is the direct object 'foundation'. شاه رش appears as a following noun phrase identifying whose foundation it is (Shah Rash); رش here is likely part of a name or epithet rather than the common noun senses listed, so the phrase functions as a possessive/genitive-like apposition.
segments.s743-g42: همان is a demonstrative 'that/same' pointing back to the named king. شاه رش repeats the name/epithet. پنج کرده is a perfect participle construction: پنج (five) + کرده ('made/done'), i.e. 'had made five (of them)'. برش here is lexically ambiguous in the list; in this sentence it functions syntactically as a noun/pronoun reference to 'them/it' or as part of a phrase meaning 'of it'—the line is elliptical and omits an explicit object, so برش must be read in context as a referent.
segments.s743-g43: ز ... و ز ... is a poetic prepositional series meaning 'from/of ... and from ...' (here: 'of stone and of plaster'). بود is the past copula 'was' linking the prepositional phrase to the noun phrase بنیاد کار 'foundation-work' (بنیاد modifying کار). Word order places the material phrases before the copula and the head noun.
segments.s743-g44: چنین 'thus/so' modifies the following necessity clause. باید is a modal/impersonal 'it is necessary' followed by a finite verb (دهد) expressing what must happen. آن کو is an archaic/elliptical sequence: آن 'that' + کو here functions as a relative/indirect interrogative/archaic marker ('who/whom' or 'that whom') — its precise role is idiomatic. دهد is the present/subjunctive (classical usage after باید) 'may/should give'. داد before کار is the noun 'justice' (or 'justness') modifying کار, i.e. 'give just work/reward.'
segments.s743-g45: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. دیوار is the subject 'wall', and ایوانش is the possessed noun 'his iwan/porch' (ایوان + pronominal suffix -ش). آمد is the past tense 'came'; به جای is an idiom meaning 'into place' or 'into its position' (به + جای). The sequence is a classical SVO/SVC order with the time/condition introduced by چو.
segments.s743-g46: بیامد is another past-form verb 'he came' (here synonymous with آمد). به پیش is a prepositional phrase 'before/in the presence of'. جهان‌کد‌خدای is a compound title/name ('ruler/chief of the world') functioning as the object of the preposition. Word order: verb + prepositional phrase marking the addressee/recipient of the action.
segments.s743-g47: که‌گر is the conditional 'if'. شاه is the subject 'the king'. بیند is a present/factual form 'sees' (classical subjunctive/indicative present used in protasis). یکی کاردان is an indefinite noun phrase 'a certain/skilled man' (یکی = 'one/some', کاردان = 'expert/skilled'); in classical Persian the indefinite often follows the verb.
segments.s743-g48: This short clause consists of modifiers describing the skilled man: گذشته is a past participle 'having passed' or 'experienced/past', followed by برو سال which here functions as an archaic temporal phrase meaning 'many years' or 'through years' (برو is archaic/poetic and not the ordinary 'go' meaning in this collocation). و introduces بسیاردان 'very learned' (an adjective/epithet). Grammatically, the sequence modifies 'یکی کاردان' earlier: 'a skilled man, having passed many years and very learned.'
